title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second in 5-on-5 goals for the Bruins this season with 13 goals.
Despite Arvidsson's pending free agency, the Bruins retained him through the trade deadline to maintain team stability.
Arvidsson's potential three-year extension could provide continuity for the Bruins amid a retooling phase.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots traded center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears, prompting a need for a new left guard.
Jared Wilson is expected to move from left guard to center, his college position, for the 2026 season.
Potential replacements include free agents Isaac Seumalo and David Edwards, and draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Make-A-Wish Foundation.
Perry aims to honor and support Make-A-Wish children in the Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island region through his marathon efforts.
He has been involved with Make-A-Wish for 15 years, inspired by the resilience and optimism of the children.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots projected to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe on a three-year, $48 million deal with $25 million guaranteed.
Boye Mafe recorded a 17.4% pass rush win rate, the highest among Patriots edge rushers last season, despite only two sacks in 2025.
Patriots unlikely to sign Colts' wide receiver Alec Pierce, who is expected to join the Chargers on a three-year, $80 million deal.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at House of Blues, Boston on March 6, 2026, with satirical songs addressing political and social issues.
Welles' setlist included original songs like 'Sometimes You Bomb Iran' and covers of 'Paranoid' and 'Take Me Home, Country Roads'.
The concert featured a mix of country and rock with sharp commentary on war, social media culture, and political figures.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a 387-foot home run and drove in three runs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea.
Japan advanced to 2-0 in World Baseball Classic pool play, with Yoshida contributing to a strong performance.
Patriots QB Drake Maye attended the game at Tokyo Dome, spending a week in Japan with his wife.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026 to support Mass Eye and Ear.
She has been a patient at Mass Eye and Ear for nearly 10 years, managing two eye conditions.
Aboujaoude-Quirk grew up in Roslindale and teaches in Boston Public Schools, fulfilling a lifelong dream to run the marathon.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler, ESPN's longest-tenured NBA analyst, joined ABC's top broadcast team in August 2025.
Legler works alongside Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson, covering high-profile NBA games including the Finals.
ESPN/ABC appointed Legler after departures of Doc Rivers and JJ Redick and a reshuffle of broadcast teams.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Cooper Flagg scored 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists in his first TD Garden NBA game with the Mavericks.
Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after an Achilles tear, scoring 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in 298 days.
Flagg, a Maine native, received a warm reception at TD Garden, fulfilling a lifelong dream to play there.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned after nearly 300 days, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in a win over the Mavericks.
Charles Barkley was impressed by Tatum's return but still favors the Cavaliers, led by James Harden, in the Eastern Conference.
Boston Celtics have the most NBA wins since January 1, tied with the Spurs, but Barkley remains skeptical of their conference dominance.
